knowledge_components.name:
Square roots represent the inverse operation of squaring:
The square root of a number is the value that, when multiplied by itself, gives the original number

complicators.name: Find square roots of perfect square integers
Find square roots of perfect square fractions and decimals
Understand square root notation and properties, including negative square roots and ±solutions
Simplify radical expressions involving perfect squares using absolute value to capture two possible solutions

knowledge_components.name:
Other roots extend the concept beyond square roots:
Cube roots, fourth roots, and higher-order roots follow similar inverse relationships

complicators.name: Find cube roots of perfect cube integers and simple fractions
Compare behavior of even roots vs odd roots with positive and negative numbers
Work with fourth roots and fifth roots of appropriate perfect powers

knowledge_components.name:
Estimating roots involves using perfect powers as reference points:
For non-perfect powers, identify nearby perfect powers and refine estimates for any root type

complicators.name: Identify which perfect powers a non-perfect root falls between for square roots and cube roots
Develop approximation strategies. Understand how to discern levels of approximation and differentiate from exact values.

knowledge_components.name:
Real numbers are classified as either rational or irrational based on whether they can be expressed as ratios of integers:
Rational numbers can be written as a/b where a and b are integers and b ≠ 0; irrational numbers cannot

complicators.name: Classify basic number forms as rational and express them as ratios when needed
Identify irrational numbers and distinguish them from similar-looking rational numbers
Use decimal patterns to classify numbers as rational or irrational

knowledge_components.name:
Root operations follow specific rules for multiplication, addition, and simplification:
Understanding when roots can be combined or separated

complicators.name: Apply product and quotient rules with perfect squares
Work with variables in perfect square expressions
Add and subtract like radicals, recognizing when radicals cannot be combined
Simplify radicals by factoring out perfect squares and reducing expressions
Understand properties of operations between rational and irrational numbers
